title: pod checkpoint/restore - incomplete restore?
body:
new e2e test flake:
```
           [It] podman checkpoint and restore container out of and into pod (net,uts,pid)
...
           # podman [options] pod create --name test_pod --share net,uts,pid
           2d3a6acebc57859090f43a10ada336d7112ab1370e9b58d0147e8b31e5bafc1b
           # podman [options] container restore --pod 2d3a6acebc57859090f43a10ada336d7112ab1370e9b58d0147e8b31e5bafc1b -i /tmp/CI_q0NH/podman-e2e-162141281/subtest-687432410/checkpoint-e0242610380e2cacb60e6b5b344a4762ddc945a6eb0f968bb9cfbb2b31dec9ec.tar.gz
           e0242610380e2cacb60e6b5b344a4762ddc945a6eb0f968bb9cfbb2b31dec9ec
           # podman [options] ps -q
           5a3fadf99b88
...
   FAILS - expected two lines of output
```
Seen twice in the last few days. It could easily be a bug in the test itself: please note that **pod checkpoint/restore is new** as of crun-1.18. This is a test that has been skipped for years, except for runc.


* fedora-41 : int podman fedora-41 root host sqlite
  * PR #24560
    * [11-14 06:57](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5232547683106816/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-and-restore-container-out-of-and-into-pod--net-uts-pid---1) in Podman checkpoint podman checkpoint and restore container out of and into pod (net,uts,pid)
  * PR #24534
    * [11-11 17:09](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4547648340885504/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-and-restore-container-out-of-and-into-pod--net-uts-pid---1) in Podman checkpoint podman checkpoint and restore container out of and into pod (net,uts,pid)

| int(2) | podman(2) | fedora-41(2) | root(2) | host(2) | sqlite(2) |

comments.body:
This actually reproduces reliably on my f40 laptop with `crun-1.18.2-1` from updates-testing. I think this is only working in CI by some miracle of slow-CI-VM timing. 100% reliable reproducer:
```bash
#!/bin/bash

set -e

# Clean slate
bin/podman rm -f -a -t0
bin/podman pod rm -a

bin/podman pod create --name foo --share net,uts,pid
bin/podman run -d --pod foo --name c_foo quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 top
bin/podman container checkpoint -e /tmp/foo.tar.gz c_foo

# Create a new pod
bin/podman pod rm foo
bin/podman pod create --name foo --share net,uts,pid
bin/podman container restore --pod foo -i /tmp/foo.tar.gz

# There should be two containers running: infra and top.
echo
bin/podman ps -a
```
Invocation:
```console
$ sudo ./foo.sh
775e8b2b618eec71b4be130605b5a854ab6c6493c1e7b7c082a75b88bb7ae991
799179c00f1283c30e0620dddcf5baeca4a3ee7fbeaa624fbe57c5293f16fc0f
e905309a80bd8bb6fe4a8f8d528e4e664a12a57bc787e02d895e26b8ed33f04e
c_foo
799179c00f1283c30e0620dddcf5baeca4a3ee7fbeaa624fbe57c5293f16fc0f
40ec4511d1d451704b86ce4d8daa85c358913c6f7e718a9bb5edf1a34888d848
e905309a80bd8bb6fe4a8f8d528e4e664a12a57bc787e02d895e26b8ed33f04e

CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                        COMMAND     CREATED                 STATUS                             PORTS       NAMES
f831fcc8620c  localhost/podman-pause:5.4.0-dev-1731614350              Less than a second ago  Up Less than a second                          40ec4511d1d4-infra
e905309a80bd  quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011            top         Less than a second ago  Exited (1) Less than a second ago              c_foo
```
...and then what I think might be a hint:
```console
$ sudo bin/podman logs e905
top: can't open '/proc': Permission denied
```
@rst0git PTAL
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I was able to replicate this error locally and it looks like `crun` doesn't use the correct SELinux mount context when creating mounts such as `/proc` for the restored container.

I will try to open a pull request with a fix for this problem soon.

cc: @giuseppe

@edsantiago The same error occurs with both `runc` and `crun`.

It looks like Podman (incorrectly) restores the SELinux labels from the checkpoint, instead of specifying the SELinux labels of the new Pod for the restored container.

Test is skipped with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25856/commits/0849cbcf83c355e8d36399e823e178f073750c70 as it is flaking to much, if someone fixes the bug please make sure to remove the skip again.
